name: Islamic Relief USA
mission: Islamic Relief USA provides relief and development in a dignified manner regardless of gender, race, or religion, and works to empower individuals in their communities and give them a voice in the world.
theoryOfChange: Our work is founded on enabling people and institutions to fulfill the rights of the poor and vulnerable. We work to empower the dispossessed toward realizing their God-given human potential and developing their capabilities and resources.

scoreSummary: Islamic Relief USA shows exceptional alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Award for Peace and Fellowship (Interreligious Forum of the Americas (FIDELA))

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://irusa.org/zakat (Source: https://irusa.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 44/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, humanitarian cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; good program ratio (≥75%) suggests reasonable delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.

judge_issues.message: The charity claims to serve 'fuqara' (poor) and 'masakin' (needy), which aligns with their humanitarian aid programs. However, the provided information does not explicitly detail the extent to which beneficiaries are exclusively within the 'fuqara' category versus other asnaf categories.

The charity self-claims zakat eligibility and provides evidence of serving multiple asnaf categories (fuqara, masakin, ibn_sabil, fisabilillah). The wallet tag 'ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE' appears appropriate based on this self-declaration and the nature of their programs.
Jargon detected in strategic narrative: 'multiplier effect'
Strengths are generic and could apply to many charities.
Strengths are generic and could apply to many charities.
The 'best for summary' could be more specific about the types of impact or programs the donor would be supporting.
The 'best for summary' could be more specific about the types of humanitarian relief or the scale of operations.
The 'case against' summary is a bit of a softball and could be more direct.

score_details.score_summary: Islamic Relief USA shows exceptional alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance.

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://irusa.org/zakat (Source: https://irusa.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: Islamic Relief USA has a 97.0/100 rating and spends 80.3% on programs.
The charity is zakat-eligible and follows zakat policy categories.
The organization reported total revenue of $147,232,658.
Programs include food parcels, hot meals, and restoring health services.
The charity provides multiple options and methods for donors to give.
Islamic Relief USA offers various opportunities for people to volunteer.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization shows strong financial management and clear transparency. While it provides direct services like food and healthcare, more detailed data on long-term outcomes would further prove its effectiveness.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public data on specific long-term cost per beneficiary
High reliance on emergency humanitarian aid which can fluctuate based on global crises

dimension_explanations.alignment: This charity is a strong fit for Muslim donors because it is zakat-eligible and follows specific religious guidelines for giving [2]. It addresses urgent humanitarian needs and offers many ways for supporters to get involved through donations or volunteering [5][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: Islamic Relief USA focuses on direct services such as delivering food parcels and restoring health services [4]. The charity spends 80.3% of its budget on programs, which is a high rate of spending on actual aid [1][3]. However, specific data on the exact cost to help each person is not fully available in public records [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Islamic Relief USA provides humanitarian aid and poverty relief across the globe and within the United States.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 80.3% ensures most funds reach those in need
Strong financial health with 18.0 months of working capital
Low fundraising costs of only $0.19 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: Islamic Relief USA is a non-profit organization that works to end poverty through disaster response and programs for vulnerable families [1][4]. The charity reported total revenue of $147,232,658 and maintains a high level of financial accountability [1][3].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a high overall GMG score of 81/100 due to its perfect financial transparency rating and strong alignment with humanitarian causes.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization saw an <cite id="4">11% decline in revenue</cite> between 2023 and 2024, which may impact future program scaling.
While IRUSA tracks outcomes, it only reports <cite id="18">two specific metrics via Candid</cite>, leaving room for more comprehensive public data.

bbb_assessment.summary: Currently does not meet BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards for charity accountability.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains high reserves to weather revenue fluctuations.
risk_factors: <cite id="4">Revenue declined from $165M to $147M in one year</cite>
<cite id="20">Does not currently meet all 20 BBB standards</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concern is a <cite id="4">recent 11% drop in annual revenue</cite> and a <cite id="20">lack of current BBB accreditation</cite>.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023 and 2024; Impact data from 2025 catalog
confidence.notes: Strong data availability across financial and program dimensions.

alignment.explanation: The organization is a <cite id="9">verified zakat-eligible entity</cite> that serves <cite id="22">Islamic asnaf categories</cite> including the poor and wayfarers.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust IRUSA due to its <cite id="3">100.0/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite> and <cite id="19">100% independent board</cite>. The charity also holds the <cite id="10">Candid Platinum Seal</cite>, the highest level of transparency.
credibility.improvement: To maximize credibility, the charity should address its <cite id="20">BBB Wise Giving Alliance status</cite> to ensure all 20 standards are publicly met.

impact.explanation: IRUSA operates with <cite id="5">80.3% program efficiency</cite> and a low <cite id="6">$0.17 fundraising cost</cite>. They deliver <cite id="7">direct services</cite> across 40+ countries.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="21">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for better cost-per-beneficiary analysis.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Documented Theory of Change with some outcome tracking

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes significant funding to international partners for direct relief delivery.

rich_narrative.headline: Islamic Relief USA provides comprehensive humanitarian aid and poverty alleviation programs both domestically and internationally while maintaining a perfect financial health rating.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fulfill their zakat through a large, established institution with a perfect transparency record and global reach.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure 100% zakat compliance through verified asnaf categories
Value high third-party ratings like Charity Navigator's 100/100
Prefer an organization with a massive global footprint in Muslim-majority regions
giving_considerations: Gifts support a large-scale operation rather than a small, local project
The organization uses a mix of direct service and grantmaking to partners
High financial reserves mean the charity is stable but not in an immediate cash crisis
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to support small, grassroots startups or those who require RCT-level academic evidence for every program.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects strong internal tracking and a documented theory of change, but lacks independent experimental evaluations like RCTs.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IRUSA uses a multi-sector approach (WASH, food, health) to stabilize communities in crisis and provide long-term pathways out of poverty.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that large-scale spending actually translates into the claimed number of families escaping poverty.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite high reserves, the scale of global humanitarian crises in regions like Gaza and Sudan exceeds current funding.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Africa, US, Europe, Asia, South America

peer_comparison.differentiator: IRUSA maintains a <cite id="11">20-month working capital reserve</cite>, which is significantly higher than many peers.

strengths.detail: The organization holds <cite id="3">a perfect 100/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
IRUSA maintains <cite id="11">18.0 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant stability during economic or humanitarian shifts.
The charity directs <cite id="5">80.3% of its revenue to programs</cite>, exceeding the <cite id="12">75% industry standard</cite> for non-profits.

strengths_deep_dive: Islamic Relief USA demonstrates a high capacity for large-scale aid delivery. In 2025, the organization reported reaching <cite id="13">420,204 direct beneficiaries through medical sector fortification</cite>. Their impact is also evident in specific regional projects, such as providing <cite id="14">WASH services to 38,400 people in Ethiopia</cite> and supporting <cite id="15">168,175 people in Afghanistan</cite>. Beyond immediate relief, IRUSA focuses on sustainability, claiming to have <cite id="16">pulled 13,500 families out of the poverty cycle</cite>. This scale is made possible by a robust grantmaking operation that distributed <cite id="17">over $252 million in foreign grants</cite> to implementing partners worldwide.
rich_narrative.summary: Islamic Relief USA (IRUSA) is a major humanitarian institution that has operated since <cite id="1">its founding in 1993</cite>. The organization manages a massive global portfolio, reporting <cite id="2">$165,235,589 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. IRUSA maintains <cite id="3">a perfect 100/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, which reflects its high standards for accountability and financial health. While the organization is a powerhouse in international relief, it recently faced a <cite id="4">revenue decline of 11% in 2024</cite>, dropping to approximately $147 million. Despite this fluctuation, IRUSA remains highly efficient, spending <cite id="5">80.3% of its total revenue on program expenses</cite>. This efficiency is supported by a strong fundraising model that costs only <cite id="6">$0.17 to raise every $1</cite>. The charity provides a wide range of services, from <cite id="7">emergency response and orphan sponsorship</cite> to long-term development. Their reach is truly global, serving regions including <cite id="8">Africa, Asia, Europe, and the United States</cite>. For Muslim donors, IRUSA offers a <cite id="9">verified zakat-eligible platform</cite> that directs funds to specific categories like the poor and needy.
